perturbation.original_text: 6. Ownership . As between the Parties, all Confidential Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Recipient agrees not to assert any claim of ownership to the Confidential In formation of the Disclosing Party or any portion thereof. Nothing set forth in this Agreement shall require Parties to share any Confidential Information with the other, and the Disclosing Party makes no representation or warranty as to the accuracy or co mpleteness of any Confidential Information disclosed hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Ownership . As between the Parties, all Confidential Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party, *except for derivative works created by Recipient*. Recipient agrees not to assert any claim of ownership to the Confidential In formation of the Disclosing Party or any portion thereof. Nothing set forth in this Agreement shall require Parties to share any Confidential Information with the other, and the Disclosing Party makes no representation or warranty as to the accuracy or co mpleteness of any Confidential Information disclosed hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of '*except for derivative works created by Recipient*' introduces a significant contradiction regarding ownership in Section 6. While the initial statement clearly designates all Confidential Information as the property of the Disclosing Party, this addition carves out an exception for derivative works. This inserted perturbation is contradicting the Ownership section. Now, the Recipient can potentially claim ownership of derivative works, blurring the lines of ownership and potentially leading to disputes over intellectual property rights stemming from the original Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Ownership . As between the Parties, all Confidential Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party, *except for derivative works created by Recipient*. Recipient agrees not to assert any claim of ownership to the Confidential In formation of the Disclosing Party or any portion thereof. Nothing set forth in this Agreement shall require Parties to share any Confidential Information with the other, and the Disclosing Party makes no representation or warranty as to the accuracy or co mpleteness of any Confidential Information disclosed hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 6 regarding ownership of confidential information. The original text clearly states that all Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party. The changed text adds "except for derivative works created by Recipient." This addition directly contradicts the initial statement by allowing the Recipient to potentially claim ownership of derivative works, leading to a conflict of ownership within the same section of the contract, thus it is a strong in-text contradiction. The Contradicted Text is in the same location as the changed Text, but there is still a strong contradiction as the same section has directly opposing concepts.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Survival . The foregoing obligations of all Parties shall continue for a period of two (2) years from the date of last disclosure of Confidential Information by either party, unless a specific portion of the Confidential FSMA FSMA Approved M utual Confidentiality 2013.05.24 3 Information disclosed becomes generally known to the public before the expiration of such two (2) year period through no fault of the Receiving Party . However, with respect to trade secrets, such obligations will survive for so long as such Confidential Information constitutes a trade secret under the Uniform Trade Secrets Act.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Survival . The foregoing obligations of all Parties shall continue for a period of two (2) years from the date of last disclosure of Confidential Information by either party, unless a specific portion of the Confidential FSMA FSMA Approved M utual Confidentiality 2013.05.24 3 Information disclosed becomes generally known to the public before the expiration of such two (2) year period through no fault of the Receiving Party *or unless otherwise agreed in writing*. However, with respect to trade secrets, such obligations will survive for so long as such Confidential Information constitutes a trade secret under the Uniform Trade Secrets Act.
perturbation.explanation: By adding '*or unless otherwise agreed in writing*' in Section 8, the survival clause is weakened and creates a contradiction. The original clause specifies a two-year survival period, with an exception for publicly known information and a longer duration for trade secrets. This inserted perturbation allowing the parties to agree to a different survival period in writing introduces an element of uncertainty. Now contradicting the text, making the two-year period not mandatory. This undermines the clarity and enforceability of the survival clause, potentially leading to disputes over the duration of confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Survival . The foregoing obligations of all Parties shall continue for a period of two (2) years from the date of last disclosure of Confidential Information by either party, unless a specific portion of the Confidential FSMA FSMA Approved M utual Confidentiality 2013.05.24 3 Information disclosed becomes generally known to the public before the expiration of such two (2) year period through no fault of the Receiving Party *or unless otherwise agreed in writing*. However, with respect to trade secrets, such obligations will survive for so long as such Confidential Information constitutes a trade secret under the Uniform Trade Secrets Act.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds '*or unless otherwise agreed in writing*' to the survival clause, which initially specified a two-year survival period. This addition introduces an ambiguity, allowing parties to deviate from the original two-year term through a written agreement. This directly contradicts the initial clause by weakening its enforceability and creating uncertainty, as the survival period is no longer mandatory. Thus, it generates a strong in-text contradiction within the document, following the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Governing Law . THIS AGREEMENT SHALL BE GOVERNE D BY THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF THE PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS OF DISTRIBUTOR AS SET FORTH IN THE FIRST PARAGRAPH ABOVE (REGARDLESS OF THE LAWS THAT MIGHT OTHERWISE GOVERN UNDER APPLICABLE PRINCIPLES OF CONFLICTS OF LAW) AS TO ALL MATTERS, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO MATTERS OF VALIDITY, CONSTRUCTION, EFFECT, PERFORMANCE AND REMEDY. The county in which the principal place of business of Distributor is located as set forth in the first paragraph above and the closest state or federal court to the principal place of business of Agency shall each be a proper (but not exclusive) place of venue for any suits to enforce this Agreement, and any legal proceedings to enforce the provision s hereof may be brought in the state or f ederal Court sitting in such county or the closest state or federal court to the principal place of business of Agency . Each P arty hereby further irrevocably waives any claim that any such court lacks jurisdiction over it, and agrees not to plead or claim, in any legal action or proceeding wi th respect to this Agreement brought in any of the aforesaid courts, that any such court lacks jurisdiction over it or that such court is located in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Governing Law . THIS AGREEMENT SHALL BE GOVERNE D BY THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF THE PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS OF DISTRIBUTOR AS SET FORTH IN THE FIRST PARAGRAPH ABOVE (REGARDLESS OF THE LAWS THAT MIGHT OTHERWISE GOVERN UNDER APPLICABLE PRINCIPLES OF CONFLICTS OF LAW) AS TO ALL MATTERS, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO MATTERS OF VALIDITY, CONSTRUCTION, EFFECT, PERFORMANCE AND REMEDY, *unless the Agency's principal place of business is in a different state, in which case that state's laws will govern*. The county in which the principal place of business of Distributor is located as set forth in the first paragraph above and the closest state or federal court to the principal place of business of Agency shall each be a proper (but not exclusive) place of venue for any suits to enforce this Agreement, and any legal proceedings to enforce the provision s hereof may be brought in the state or f ederal Court sitting in such county or the closest state or federal court to the principal place of business of Agency . Each P arty hereby further irrevocably waives any claim that any such court lacks jurisdiction over it, and agrees not to plead or claim, in any legal action or proceeding wi th respect to this Agreement brought in any of the aforesaid courts, that any such court lacks jurisdiction over it or that such court is located in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of '*unless the Agency's principal place of business is in a different state, in which case that state's laws will govern*' introduces a contradiction in the governing law provision in Section 9. The initial statement specifies that the Distributor's state law governs. With this change the governing law is now depending on where the Agency place of buisness is. This inserted perturbation leads to uncertainty as it now contradicting and creates in-text ambiguity regarding which state's laws will be applied, potentially leading to forum shopping and increased litigation costs.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Governing Law . THIS AGREEMENT SHALL BE GOVERNE D BY THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF THE PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS OF DISTRIBUTOR AS SET FORTH IN THE FIRST PARAGRAPH ABOVE (REGARDLESS OF THE LAWS THAT MIGHT OTHERWISE GOVERN UNDER APPLICABLE PRINCIPLES OF CONFLICTS OF LAW) AS TO ALL MATTERS, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO MATTERS OF VALIDITY, CONSTRUCTION, EFFECT, PERFORMANCE AND REMEDY, *unless the Agency's principal place of business is in a different state, in which case that state's laws will govern*. The county in which the principal place of business of Distributor is located as set forth in the first paragraph above and the closest state or federal court to the principal place of business of Agency shall each be a proper (but not exclusive) place of venue for any suits to enforce this Agreement, and any legal proceedings to enforce the provision s hereof may be brought in the state or f ederal Court sitting in such county or the closest state or federal court to the principal place of business of Agency . Each P arty hereby further irrevocably waives any claim that any such court lacks jurisdiction over it, and agrees not to plead or claim, in any legal action or proceeding wi th respect to this Agreement brought in any of the aforesaid courts, that any such court lacks jurisdiction over it or that such court is located in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a condition to the governing law clause (Section 9). Originally, the agreement was governed by the laws of the state of the Distributor's principal place of business. The addition of '*unless the Agency's principal place of business is in a different state, in which case that state's laws will govern*' introduces a direct contradiction, as it now specifies an alternative state law might govern. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty about which state's laws apply, contradicting the original intent of having a single, clear governing law, causing an in-text contradiction as defined in the perturbation explanation. Since the Contradicted Text is the Changed Text, and there is contradiction by introducing ambiguity into the text, this fits the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.
